title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
gist: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz and struck US facilities across the Gulf in retaliation for US strikes on ~140 Iranian targets, renewing pressure on global energy prices as the new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed vengeance.

insights.claim: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said the Strait of Hormuz would be closed 'until further notice,' renewing pressure on global energy prices that have already soared over hostilities around the route.
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: Direct upside catalyst for crude and LNG prices; a hard closure threatens ~a fifth of seaborne oil/LNG.

insights.claim: US CENTCOM said it hit approximately 140 targets in Iran including missile and drone sites, naval capabilities, ammunition storage, communication networks and coastal surveillance locations.
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: Scale of strikes signals sustained conflict, not a one-off; keep risk premia elevated.

insights.claim: Iran struck across the Gulf — three missiles into Jordan, a US radar site in Kuwait, carrier refueling/support facilities at Duqm in Oman, and a US base and facilities in Qatar where three were injured — plus air defenses engaging in the UAE and Bahrain.
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: Regional spread of strikes raises the geopolitical risk premium on all Gulf assets and shipping.

insights.claim: Iran's Guards said they destroyed the refuelling facilities for US aircraft carriers and logistical support centres at Oman's port of Duqm, hours after Oman hosted Iran's foreign minister.
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: Targeting US naval logistics while negotiating shows Iran mixing pressure with diplomacy.

insights.claim: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed revenge for the US-Israeli killing of his father and predecessor on February 28, the first day of the war.
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: New hardline leadership signaling vengeance lowers odds of near-term de-escalation.
insights.quote: Vengeance is the will of our nation and must inevitably be carried out

insights.claim: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth responded to Iran's Hormuz strikes with 'Iran made a poor choice. Now they pay.'
insights.speaker: Agence France-Presse
insights.so_what: US rhetoric points to continued retaliation rather than restraint.
insights.quote: Iran made a poor choice. Now they pay
